title: The role of disclosures on trust in international development
abstract: Administration costs of nonprofit organisations have long been thought to influence donation, but the affect of differing disclosure types and donor trust remain unclear. This thesis provides insight on the role of trust as an intermediary between nonprofit disclosure of administration expenditure and donation intention. A comparison of financial efficiency versus community effectiveness, and low versus high organisational performance was investigated experimentally with scenarios presented to a sample representative of the Australian population. A new moderated mediation model was developed, providing understanding for industry and academia on the impact disclosure types have on trust and how this affects donation intention.
